message:
feat(macros): add scope! state binding; re-export join!/race! proc macros from crate root

scope! macro (asupersync-macros/src/scope.rs, lib.rs, spawn.rs):

  Add an optional state: expr parameter to scope! that binds a __state
  variable visible to nested spawn! calls. Previously spawn! required an
  ambient __state binding that callers had to create manually. The new
  syntax is:

    scope!(cx, state: &mut state, { spawn!(async { work() }); })

  The parser now loops over keyword options (budget:, state:) instead of
  checking for budget: once, allowing them to appear in any order after
  the optional name. Duplicate detection rejects repeated budget: or
  state: with a clear error message.

  spawn.rs doc updated to document that __state can come from either
  scope! state: or a manual let binding.

  Tests added: parse_state_scope, parse_named_state_budget_scope,
  error_duplicate_state, generate_scope_with_state_binding.

  macro_spawn_closure.rs: replaced dummy test with a real compile-time
  verification that scope! + spawn! compose correctly through a minimal
  MiniCx/MiniScope/MiniState mock.

join!/race! proc-macro re-exports (src/lib.rs, src/combinator/join.rs,
src/combinator/race.rs):

  Re-export join! and race! proc macros from the crate root alongside
  scope!, spawn!, and join_all! when the proc-macros feature is enabled.
  The fallback compile_error! macro_rules! placeholders now have
  #[cfg(not(feature = "proc-macros"))] gates so they don't conflict with
  the proc macro re-exports. Doc comments on the placeholders updated to
  point users to the proc-macro versions.

Examples updated:
  - Import path changed from asupersync::proc_macros:: to asupersync::
  - let _ = replaced with std::mem::drop() to silence unused-result
  - Clippy allows added for trivially_copy_pass_by_ref, unused_self,
    unnecessary_wraps
  - macros_race.rs: added named-branch + timeout race variant demo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
